prompt: Small hardening fix in nix/valley-host.nix's backup rendering, learned from a real failure: the restic sftp args the module renders (currently -i <key> -o UserKnownHostsFile=<file> -o IdentitiesOnly=yes) do NOT set BatchMode, so when anything tries to prompt (unknown host key, key passphrase), root's ssh hangs until systemd gives up — a 54-second opaque timeout instead of a 2-second explicit error. Add '-o BatchMode=yes' to the rendered sftp args in the module (find where sftp.args is composed for services.restic.backups). Also add '-o StrictHostKeyChecking=yes' if not already implied, so an unpinned host key is an immediate clear failure. Keep the change minimal and in the module's comment style — one short comment noting why (no prompts exist in a systemd unit; fail fast). Update the module-eval flake check that greps the rendered unit to also pin the BatchMode flag. Verify: nix flake check passes. PR body: the failure story in two sentences (54s hang on init when the known_hosts pin was in doubt; generic SSH_FX_FAILURE masked the real error) and what the flags change.
